method.population_source: U.S. Census Bureau Vintage 2025 incorporated-place estimates
method.selection: Next sixteen largest incorporated U.S. cities above 100,000 population not already represented by BID Atlas or included in the first five large-city batches
method.active_hit_definition: At least one currently operating assessment-funded BID or locally named legal equivalent supported by an official source
method.notes: Candidate discovery and production boundary ingestion are performed together where an active district is verified. TIF-only, CRA-only, entertainment, residential, and capital-infrastructure districts are excluded. Cities already represented by a mapped district were skipped. Pasadena, California (Census rank 216) was skipped because its active districts are already represented.

records.local_term: Proposed downtown business improvement district (not formed)
records.summary: No operating assessment-funded BID or tourism district was verified in Orange. City planning records proposed a self-funded downtown BID in the late 1990s, but current downtown cleaning, parking, economic-development, and event programs are procured or funded directly by the City and no formation resolution, management district plan, assessment levy, or active district operator was identified.
records.production_next_action: Monitor for a current downtown or lodging assessment formation proceeding; do not map the historic district, downtown planning area, or City-funded service area.
